behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: pure grouping logic over input records (trace_id + service fields), no required I/O or OS behavior. Pass 2 mapping: pure logic => Unit. MOCK-MISS check: this behavior can fail inside one grouping function; module wiring is not required to expose it. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration/System because behavior text only requires correct grouping by trace ID across records, not real transport/storage/process boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Call the grouping function with mixed-service log records sharing and not sharing trace IDs; assert groups are keyed only by trace ID and include entries from different services in the same group.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: timestamp ordering/skew-adjustment algorithm. Pass 2 mapping: algorithmic ordering without real OS/network/model dependencies => Unit. MOCK-HIDE check: mocking does not inherently hide this failure because the failure is in sorting/skew math itself. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration/System because behavior text focuses on ordering correctness under 500ms skew, which is testable with synthetic timestamps and service IDs.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the timeline ordering logic with crafted cross-service entries whose raw timestamps would misorder without skew handling; assert final order is correct when skew is <=500ms and boundary cases at exactly 500ms are handled as specified.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: local module interaction between stats computation (baseline + p99) and anomaly report generation. Pass 2 mapping: multi-module in-process wiring => Integration. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required, (b) no AI/ML output dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text specifies deterministic threshold logic and report flagging, not external infra or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run the anomaly pipeline in-process with a dataset where computed p99 crosses 3x baseline; assert the report includes a latency-spike flag and relevant metric values. Include a near-threshold control case that should not flag.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: parser error handling + pipeline continuation + aggregate skipped-count reporting across modules. Pass 2 mapping: this is a module-boundary resilience behavior => Integration. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text, (b) no AI/ML dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is about malformed entry handling semantics, which can be exposed with in-process input streams/fakes.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the ingestion pipeline a mixed batch of valid and malformed JSON records; assert process completes, valid records continue through, malformed ones are skipped, and reported skipped count matches the malformed input count.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: runtime throughput/backpressure behavior under sustained burst, which depends on real process scheduling, buffering, and timing characteristics. Pass 2 mapping: requires real environment behavior => System. MOCK-HIDE check: in-process mocks/stubs can hide queue saturation, scheduler delays, and real timing bottlenecks. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text is explicit about production-rate burst handling (10,000/s) and no-drop/no-lag guarantees, which require real runtime performance validation.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual tool process with a real high-rate log generator (10,000 entries/s) for a sustained interval; verify ingest count equals sent count, processing lag stays within defined bound, and no backlog growth indicates falling behind.
